about_the_product.description: The new NZXT Gold PSUs offer improved performance, adhering to Intel ATX 3.1 standards for modern GPUs and power stability. Redesigned with superior components, increased fan airflow, and a longer chassis, they deliver higher efficiency, enhanced reliability, and reduced noise.

items.title: NZXT C850 SFX Gold review: a compact next‑gen PSU for powerful SFF builds

user_reviews.title: Works as described.
user_reviews.text: Picked up from Best Buy for $119 3-14-2026. Very well built and comes with a purple NZXT bag for all the cables to be stored in. Zero Fan button needs to be pushed in for that function to work (button in, fan off). If temp comes up, fan will automatically turn on - Zero Fan function works great - Very Quiet. This C850 Gold ATX 3.1 unit replaced our 2017 ABS MJ1100-M 80 Gold, not because it failed, but because we upgraded our GPU to the RTX5080 OC (360watt) and needed the 12v-2x6 connector. NZXT PSU fit into our existing case leaving more room than my old PSU, cables are slim and modular (clean look).

user_reviews.title: Nice Supply, but one big noisy problem
user_reviews.text: Fit in case well. Installed in Old Antex 900 v2 case. Had a big problem though. The fan rubbed on top of power supply case. Didn`t have time to return so I went ahead and violated the warranty and fixed fan. The fan switch on back by power switch is a very confusing addition. You will see when you download manual and read how it is supposed to work. If you press it in, fan runs and when you leave it out, it`s automatic based on load. Why not just get rid of switch. Way too confusing. Even when running at 50% load, it's still very quiet. As for operation, works well and problem with old supply, high noise levels caused by failing high voltage capacitors, is now gone. Would buy again. Maybe next time, if fan rubbing case is a problem will return for a replacement.

user_reviews.title: Best bang for your buck
user_reviews.text: Bought this because I upgraded to a 5070ti and my factory 650w psu was not gonna cut it. Came with every cable i needed (in cool case too)and the install was extremely simple even if you're not a pc builder. Took maybe 15 minutes to swap it out and since its fully modular, you only hook up the cables you need. Its so convenient. Came with a 12vhpwr cable too so you don't need an adapter for newer gpus. Makes no noise from my experience and had a zero fan button to keep noise down even more. Love it and will highly recommend it for someone whos upgrading to a current gen gpu and needs the power.

user_reviews.title: If you have a mid-high tier setup get this PSU
user_reviews.text: I recommend this PSU to anyone who needs a good value, good quality, easy to install PSU. not only is it fully modular, it looks good and has functionality that will keep your build running safer than using a different PSU. If you have an expensive setup DO NOT cheap out on a 80+ Bronze cheap PSU that barely keeps your build safe. If you have a RTX 5070 Ti like me I recommend a 80+ Gold.

user_reviews.title: Dead on arrival, won’t boot at all.
user_reviews.text: As another reviewer mentioned it was dead on arrival for me as well. I took the chance after seeing the other review, because the reviews on Amazon for it were very good. It didn’t boot whatsoever for me had absolutely no signs power. Its an actual brick. I’m not sure if Best Buy got a bad batch or what, nzxt is usually a trustworthy company.

user_reviews.title: High quality and easy to run the cables
user_reviews.text: High quality PSU, stays quiet, cool and delivers steady voltage. Fully modular cables were easy to work with!

user_reviews.title: In NZXT c850 ATX 3.1
user_reviews.text: I've never used NZXT but I love how they are set up and how they are 3.1 ATX with three separate graphics card cables.. I just don't understand why there's a button in the back to turn the fan on and off.. shuldnt the fan always be on, idk nzxt isn't the only one that has on and off button for the fan though.. I just don't get it but other than that I would suggest in NZXT power supply units to anybody..
